g.text: STEP 1 · HTTP Request
POST /v1/chat/completions
multimodal/entrypoint/http_server.py
Receives multimodal chat request: messages (with image_url/base64), sampling_params, ...
STEP 2 · Multimodal Tokenization
MultimodalTokenizer.generate()
multimodal/manager/multimodal_tokenizer.py
• Convert prompt to token IDs
• Build TokenizedGenerateMMReqInput, send to GlobalScheduler over ZMQ
STEP 3 · GlobalScheduler Receives Request
GlobalScheduler.event_loop()
multimodal/manager/global_scheduler.py
• Identify request type via TypeBasedDispatcher
• Call convert_request() to create a Req object
• Add request to req_store (rid → ReqTrackingState mapping)
• Push request into Stage 0's in_queue
ReqTrackingState tracks the current Stage index of the request
STEP 4 · Stage 0 - ViT Encoder
VitScheduler.event_loop_normal()
multimodal/manager/scheduler/vit_scheduler.py
• Fetch requests via communication_backend.recv_requests() (i.e. in_queue)
• Run ViT model to extract image embeddings: Qwen2_5_VL_VisionModel
• vit_worker.forward(req) updates req in place (attaches visual features)
• Forward via communication_backend.send_pyobj(req) (i.e. out_queue)
Independent Device Mesh, pipelined in parallel with the AR Stage
STEP 5 · Stage 1 - AR (Autoregressive Generation)
Scheduler.event_loop_normal() (via auto_regressive alias)
srt/managers/scheduler.py (reuses text engine Scheduler)
• Fetch requests with visual features via communication_backend.recv_requests()
• Concatenate visual token embeddings with text token embeddings
• Enter Qwen2_5_VL_Generation text decoding: Prefill → Decode loop
• Sample next text token each step until EOS / max_tokens
• Emit generated token IDs via send_pyobj() (i.e. out_queue)
Reuses LLM Continuous Batching, KV Cache, and sampling stack
STEP 6 · GlobalScheduler Collects Output
GlobalScheduler reads results from Stage 1 out_queue
multimodal/manager/global_scheduler.py
• Remove request from req_store
• Build BatchTokenIDOut and send to Detokenizer over ZMQ
Stage 1 is configured with final_output=true, marking the Pipeline endpoint
STEP 7 · Multimodal Detokenizer
MultimodalDetokenizer.event_loop()
multimodal/manager/multimodal_detokenizer.py
• Receive token IDs and incrementally decode them to text
• Return result to TokenizerManager and finally to client via HTTP response
Key Design Notes
Stage Pipeline Architecture
• Each Stage owns its own Scheduler, Model Executor, and Device Mesh
• Stages talk via communication_backend (backed by queue.Queue, simplified as in_queue / out_queue)
• GlobalScheduler handles request routing and inter-Stage data forwarding
• Each Stage runs in its own thread for Pipeline parallelism
Differences from the Text Engine
• Text: single Scheduler + autoregressive loop (Prefill → Decode → Decode → ...)
• Multimodal (Qwen2.5-VL): one-shot ViT extraction → concat with text → AR Stage reuses LLM stack
• Other Pipelines (e.g. T2I): ViT → Diffusion (fixed steps) → VAE, cannot fold into AR loop
YAML-Driven Configuration
• Stage Pipeline defined by YAML files (multimodal/models/static_configs/*.yaml)
• Each model has its own Pipeline config (Qwen2.5-VL: 2-Stage; Wan T2I: 3-Stage)
• Config includes: Scheduler type, device count, model path, final_output flag, etc.
ReqTrackingState
• GlobalScheduler maintains req_store: Dict[rid, ReqTrackingState]
• Tracks the current Stage index (current_stage) for each request
• Supports Abort propagation: send AbortReq to current Stage and clean up resources
Radix Cache Disabled
• Visual Token sequences in multimodal requests rarely repeat; Prefix sharing is minimal
• Enabling Radix Cache adds lookup overhead and memory fragmentation
• Multimodal AR Stage uses lighter strategies like ChunkCache to manage KV Cache
• Text engine still defaults to Radix Cache; toggle via server_args
